english: beyond, both as to the future and the past, e.g. bskal-pa (bskal pa) graṅs-med-pai (grangs med pai) p'a-rol-na (pha rol na), innumerable Kalpas ago, frq.; p'a-rol-pa (pha rol pa), 1. one living on the other side. 2. also p'o (pho), enemy, adversary, p'a-rol-pai (pha rol pai) rgyal-p'o (rgyal pho), p'a-rol-pai (pha rol pai) dmag (dmag), p'a-rol-gyi (pha rol gyi) dmag-ts'ogs (dmag tshogs), the hostile king, hostile army; p'a-rol (pha rol) gnon-pa (gnon pa), to vanquish the enemy; p'a-rol-gyis (pha rol gyis) mi (mi) ts'ugs-par (tshugs par) gyur-ba (gyur ba), not to be molested by the enemy. 3. also p'o (pho), the other; the neighbour; p'a-rol-gyi (pha rol gyi) len-pa (len pa), to take away the neighbour's property; p'a-rol-gyi (pha rol gyi) rdzas (rdzas), yo-byad (yo byad), nor (nor), Stg.; p'a-rol (pha rol) gnon-pa (gnon pa), Tar. 12,20: excelling others, Schf. exceedingly. — Cf. also p'ar (phar) and p'an (phan) II.
Sch. wall; edge, border; in two passages of Glr. the latter meaning does not suit at all, and the former not well; rather: tile; v. pag (pag).

W., sweet dried apricots, in C. *na-ri-kám-bu* (na ri kam bu), in Hind. خوباني, in Russia bokhari, bokharki, also called Persian fruit, much exported from Balti, Kabul, and other countries of western Asia.
p'a-til (pha til) (Ar. فتيلة) W., lunt, match; *dug-c'e* (dug che), to light (a match).
puff-ball, bull-fist (a kind of fungus) Wdii.
Glr. and elsewh., C., p'a-oṅ (pha ong) Pth., Bal., p'a-loṅ (pha long) Ld., a large rock or block, above ground.

1. bat (animal) Lt., Thgy., C.; *p'o-loṅ-hel-kyi* (pho long hel kyi), *p'a-waṅ-aṅ-kyi* (pha wang ang kyi), *-ar-kyi* (ar kyi), W., *p'a-waṅ-t'ar* (pha wang thar), Sik., id.(= bya-waṅ (bya wang)). 2. rdo p'a-waṅ (rdo pha wang), ammonite.
1. also p'e-raṅ (phe rang), = *p'i-liṅ* (phi ling), C., Feringhi, European. — 2. vulg. venereal disease.
1. Lh., a coarse covering or carpet. — 2. a mountain on the other side.
rdo-rje-p'a-lam (rdo rje pha lam), diamond, Lt.
he changes colour, turns pale, with consternation, Ld.

I. v. pag (pag). — II. in B. gen. p'ag-pa (phag pa), swine, hog, pig (introduced into C. from China, and largely consumed; in W. somewhat known from India, *ri-p'ag* (ri phag) and *luś-p'ag* (lush phag) being distinguished as the wild boar and the tame hog); p'ag-pai sna (phag pai sna), Glr.; rus-pa (rus pa), Med.; bcud (?) (bcud) Lt.; p'ag-gi gdoṅ (phag gi gdong), a pig's face, Sambh.; p'a-p'ag (pha phag), not castrated, p'o-p'ag (pho phag), castrated boar; mo-p'ag (mo phag), sow. — p'ag-k'yu (phag khyu), herd of swine. — p'ag-mgo (phag mgo), 1. boar's head (a valued protective against demons, it being hid in the ground under the threshold of the door). 2. S.g. fol. 26, it seems to be a mineral used in medicine. — p'ag-rgod (phag rgod), wild boar. — p'ag-mc'e (phag mche), tusks of a boar. — p'ag-t'uṅ (phag thung), Sch.: a large boar (?). — p'ag-p'rug (phag phrug), young pig. — p'ag-ma (phag ma), Sch., gelded hog. — p'ag-mo (phag mo), 1. sow. 2. a goddess v. rdo-rje (rdo rje). — p'ag-ts'aṅ (phag tshang), pig-sty. — p'ag-ts'il (phag tshil), hog's lard; bacon. — p'ag-ts'ogs (phag tshogs) = p'ag-k'yu (phag khyu). — p'ag-rdzi (phag rdzi), swine-herd. — p'ag-zé (phag ze), hog's bristle, Wdii. — p'ag-yar-ma (phag yar ma), Sch., the fattening of pigs (?) — p'ag-ril (phag ril), pig's muck (?) Lt. — p'ag-śa (phag sha), pork. III. (Cs. p'ag-ma (phag ma)), something hidden; concealment: p'ag-na mi yod-pa (phag na mi yod pa), a man concealed behind, Dzl.; p'ag-gam gru źig-tu (phag gam gru zhig tu), in a corner, in obscurity, Dzl.; *ts'a-big p'ag-la yod* (tsha big phag la yod), it is somewhat hidden, cannot be seen well (from this place), Ld.; *p'ag-la zá-ce* (phag la za ce), to eat (dainties) by stealth, W.; nyi-ma rii p'ag-tu gro (nyi ma rii phag tu gro), Thgy., the sun hides himself behind the mountain; sgo-p'ag-nas bltas-pas (sgo phag nas bltas pas), to watch, spy, lurk behind the door, Glr., v. also jab, pa (jab pa); p'ag nyan taṅ-ce (phag nyan tang ce) W., to listen. — *p'ag-ste* (phag ste), W. ('a hidden paring-axe' v. ste-p'o (ste pho)) plane; *p'ag-ste gyab-ce* (phag ste gyab ce), *dúd-ce* (dud ce), *śrub-ce* (shrub ce), to plane. — p'ag-ts'oṅ (phag tshong), smuggling, c'o-ce (cho ce), to smuggle, W. *taṅ-k'an* (tang khan), smuggler, W. — p'ag-ra (phag ra), parapet. — p'ag-rags (phag rags), rampart, intrenchment. — p'ag-lam (phag lam), secret path (of smugglers). — *p'ag-sug* (phag sug), bribery, C., W.; *p'ag-sug taṅ-ce* (phag sug tang ce), to bribe; zá-ce (za ce), to accept a bribe, W.<
